verses.text: Isaiah praises, 'O LORD, thou art my God... thou hast done wonderful things,' overthrowing the fortified city of the proud. Judgment becomes a song. The faithful turn God's mighty acts into worship.
Isaiah praises, 'O LORD, thou art my God... thou hast done wonderful things,' overthrowing the fortified city of the proud. Judgment becomes a song. The faithful turn God's mighty acts into worship.
Isaiah praises, 'O LORD, thou art my God... thou hast done wonderful things,' overthrowing the fortified city of the proud. Judgment becomes a song. The faithful turn God's mighty acts into worship.

The LORD has been 'a strength to the poor... a refuge from the storm,' shading the needy from the blast of the terrible. He shelters the helpless. The God who topples the proud is a hiding place for the weak.

The LORD has been 'a strength to the poor... a refuge from the storm,' shading the needy from the blast of the terrible. He shelters the helpless. The God who topples the proud is a hiding place for the weak.

On His mountain He spreads a feast for all peoples, and 'will swallow up death in victory; and... wipe away tears from off all faces.' This is the gospel hope. In Christ, death is conquered and every sorrow shall be ended.

On His mountain He spreads a feast for all peoples, and 'will swallow up death in victory; and... wipe away tears from off all faces.' This is the gospel hope. In Christ, death is conquered and every sorrow shall be ended.

On His mountain He spreads a feast for all peoples, and 'will swallow up death in victory; and... wipe away tears from off all faces.' This is the gospel hope. In Christ, death is conquered and every sorrow shall be ended.

His people say, 'Lo, this is our God; we have waited for him... we will be glad in his salvation,' while the proud are trodden down. Waiting ends in joy. Those who trust and wait for God are not put to shame.
